headline: White House recalibrates Anthropic stance amid security tensions
subtitle: Government shifts on Mythos access · Gemini powers Google vehicles · ChatGPT's goblin mystery solved
intro: The White House is reversing course on its Anthropic feud as Mythos' powerful cyber capabilities force a national security recalculation. While Pentagon hardliners resist backing down, officials now see strategic value in closer collaboration with the AI maker.

bullets.text: White House blocks Anthropic's plan to expand Mythos access
bullets.summary: The White House is pushing back on Anthropic's proposal to expand Mythos access from 50 to nearly 120 firms, citing compute concerns for government use. However, a forthcoming AI memo will reportedly allow agencies to adopt multi-vendor solutions and sidestep supply chain risk designations, signaling a tactical shift in the long-running feud.

bullets.text: Google launches Gemini in cars with navigation and voice control
bullets.summary: Google is rolling out Gemini across compatible vehicles, replacing Assistant with a more conversational system for navigation, messages, music, and car settings. General Motors is adding the feature to roughly 4 million vehicles from 2022 onward, with future integrations for Gmail, Calendar, and smart home controls.

bullets.text: OpenAI traces ChatGPT's goblin fixation to single personality setting
bullets.summary: OpenAI discovered that ChatGPT's obsession with goblins, gremlins, and fantasy creatures stemmed from a reward signal in the Nerdy personality preset. After the November launch of ChatGPT-5.1, goblin mentions spiked 175%, but OpenAI retired Nerdy in March and banned creature references in GPT-5.5.

bullets.text: Stripe launches AI wallet agent with human approval gates
bullets.summary: Stripe introduced an AI wallet designed for autonomous agents to execute purchases while requiring human sign-off on every transaction. The tool integrates safety checkpoints into agent workflows for controlled commercial interactions.

bullets.text: Anthropic releases Claude Security for enterprise vulnerability scanning
bullets.summary: Anthropic launched the public beta of Claude Security, leveraging Claude Opus 4.7 to scan codebases for vulnerabilities and assist with patch generation. The tool offers enterprises automated security assessment capabilities powered by advanced reasoning.

bullets.text: Meta opens ads platform to third-party AI tools via MCP server
bullets.summary: Meta enabled advertisers to manage campaigns through Claude, Cursor, and other AI agents connected via a new MCP server. The move expands Meta's advertising ecosystem to include autonomous AI tools for campaign optimization.

bullets.text: Cloudflare agents can now create accounts and deploy domains
bullets.summary: Cloudflare introduced automation that lets agents handle account provisioning, domain registration, and deployment while keeping humans in control of permissions. This removes friction from infrastructure setup for less technical builders.

bullets.text: Stripe launches agentic commerce with agent-ready payments
bullets.summary: Stripe announced its Agentic Commerce Suite and Link wallet for agents, plus a CLI that grants agents temporary payment credentials without exposing real card details. This enables secure agent-driven transactions at scale.

bullets.text: Cursor SDK lets you build agents with Cursor's runtime
bullets.summary: Developers can now construct agents using the same models and infrastructure powering Cursor, enabling CI/CD pipelines and embedded agent workflows. This democratizes access to production-grade agent capabilities.

bullets.text: Warp goes open-source with OpenAI as founding sponsor
bullets.summary: Warp open-sourced its terminal, betting that community contributors handle taste and verification while agents handle implementation. This hybrid model could reshape how terminal tools evolve.

bullets.text: Lightfield AI-native CRM ships workflow automation
bullets.summary: Lightfield launched Skills, allowing users to define workflows in plain English and trigger them with sentences. Agents execute against your full data graph with code execution and web search.

main_story.title: White House recalibrates Anthropic stance as Mythos raises security stakes
main_story.body: The White House has spent months escalating its conflict with Anthropic, but the emergence of Mythos as a cyber-capable frontier model has forced a strategic reassessment. Officials now see Mythos as too powerful to leave entirely in private hands, yet want to restrict broader access while avoiding a complete reversal of the Pentagon's hardline position. The tension came to a head when Anthropic proposed expanding Mythos access to nearly 120 companies, prompting White House objections over compute strain. However, an incoming AI policy memo signals a softer approach, reportedly allowing agencies to adopt multi-vendor solutions and sidestep supply chain restrictions. Even as the government tries to maintain leverage, internal rifts persist—Pentagon leadership remains hostile to Anthropic, while national security officials recognize the operational value of maintaining closer ties to the company's most advanced models.
main_story.why: This shift reflects the collision between national security concerns and technological realities. Mythos' capabilities have raised the stakes beyond a policy dispute, forcing pragmatic recalibration of how the government accesses frontier AI while competing with other nations.
main_story.take: The White House is attempting to thread an impossible needle—appearing tough while actually softening. The feud may cool, but the underlying tension between government control, private innovation, and security remains unresolved.

around_the_horn.text: OpenAI already achieved its 2029 compute target of 10 GW within months
Elon Musk revealed xAI used distillation to train on OpenAI models during trial
Cursor launched Security Review for automated codebase vulnerability scanning
